macro_architecture.reasoning: Map-Reduce analysis of 4 containers with 737 total components

layer_structure: Presentation Layer (Components, Directives, Pipes, UI Modules)
Integration/Data Access Layer (Adapters, API Modules, HTTP Interceptors)

container_analyses.summary: The backend service follows a classic layered architecture built on Spring Boot, exposing a rich REST API for deed management, archiving, key handling and reporting. It implements the core business capabilities defined by the Deed Management and Archiving bounded context and integrates with external key, archiving, and reporting services as well as a security provider. While the technical foundation and functional coverage are solid, the codebase shows moderate separation of concerns, coupling, and cohesion, with notable technical debt such as oversized controllers, direct repository access from services, and anemic domain entities, resulting in an overall grade of B.

capabilities: Create, retrieve, update and delete actions
Manage deed entries and their lifecycle (creation, handover, locking, logging)
Handle document metadata storage and retrieval
Execute handover data set processing and history tracking
Manage signatures and signature information
Perform archiving operations including signing and token handling
Key management including re‑encryption and crypto‑state queries
Generate and serve business purpose data
Run scheduled jobs and background processing
Produce reports via REST endpoints
Expose a comprehensive REST API for external consumption
integrations: Potential external proxy / token authentication infrastructure

debt_indicators: Services directly accessing repositories (tight layer coupling)
High count of entity classes suggesting anemic domain model
Missing explicit service interfaces for many implementations
recommendations: Refactor oversized controllers into smaller, purpose‑specific ones
Introduce service interfaces and depend on abstractions rather than concrete implementations
Encapsulate repository access within domain services to preserve layer boundaries
Consider consolidating related entities or applying inheritance/polymorphism to reduce entity explosion
Apply DTOs and mapper layers to decouple REST contracts from domain entities
Add unit/integration test coverage for service layer to highlight hidden coupling

container_analyses.overall_assessment: The backend delivers a comprehensive set of business functionalities on a robust Spring‑based stack, but architectural and code‑quality concerns—particularly around controller size, service‑repository coupling, and domain model design—limit its maintainability and scalability. Addressing these issues will elevate the system from a solid B grade toward an A‑level solution.
top_recommendations: Refactor oversized controllers into modular, purpose‑specific controllers.
Introduce explicit service interfaces and program to abstractions to reduce coupling.
Encapsulate repository access within domain/services layers and apply DTOs to separate REST contracts from domain entities.

container_analyses.summary: The frontend container follows a component‑based Angular architecture with clear presentation, business, and integration layers, leveraging TypeScript, RxJS and the Angular CLI stack to expose a rich set of deed‑registry capabilities through REST adapters. While functional coverage is comprehensive, quality metrics reveal moderate separation of concerns, coupling and cohesion, and a high technical debt signal from the unusually large number of services, adapters and pipes, indicating opportunities to tighten abstractions and module boundaries.

layers: Presentation Layer (Components, Directives, Pipes, UI Modules)
Integration/Data Access Layer (Adapters, API Modules, HTTP Interceptors)
technical.integration_style: REST API (HTTP calls via services and adapters)

capabilities: Create, edit, view and delete deeds
Approve and validate deed signatures
Manage deed workflows (archive, finalize, change AOID, delete)
Handle document import/export (PDF, JSON)
Perform reencryption and related background jobs
Manage user authentication and authorization
Send and display notifications
Configure application settings and user preferences
Provide online help and guidance
Generate and view reports
Manage number allocation and registration
Interact with backend services via REST adapters
functional.bounded_context: Deed Registry and Legal Document Management
integrations: Various REST endpoints defined in the interface list (e.g., /deed-entries, /document, /participants, /uvz, /access-denied, etc.)

debt_indicators: Very high number of services (131) – likely some are oversized or overlapping
Large number of adapters (50) – may indicate missing higher‑level abstraction or façade
Excessive pipe count (67) – potential duplication of transformation logic
No explicit relation mapping – hard to verify proper module boundaries
recommendations: Identify and split large services into smaller, domain‑focused services
Introduce façade or gateway services to reduce direct component‑to‑adapter coupling
Consolidate similar pipes into shared utility functions or a limited set of reusable pipes
Review module imports/exports to enforce strict feature module boundaries
Add interface abstractions for adapters and services to ease testing and future replacements
Implement a linting rule set (e.g., ESLint with Angular‑specific rules) to detect cyclic dependencies and overly deep injection hierarchies

container_analyses.overall_assessment: The frontend delivers robust functional coverage for deed‑registry workflows using a solid Angular foundation, yet architectural drift—evident from the proliferation of services, adapters and pipes—creates maintainability risk. Addressing these quality concerns will improve modularity, testability and long‑term scalability.
top_recommendations: Consolidate and refactor oversized services into focused, domain‑specific units.
Introduce façade/gateway layers to decouple components from low‑level adapters.
Enforce strict feature‑module boundaries and clean up duplicate pipes.

layers: Presentation Layer (Components, Directives, Pipes, UI Modules)

executive_summary: Map-Reduce analysis completed for 4 containers with 737 components.
top_recommendations: Consider extracting large containers into separate services
Add API documentation for external interfaces
Implement health checks for all containers
